intro: The biggest real distinction between Amazon CloudFront and Yandex Cloud is straightforward: Amazon CloudFront uses freemium with a paid tier from $0/month, enterprise plan available; Yandex Cloud uses usage-based pricing, a free tier. Amazon CloudFront sits at freemium with a paid tier from $0/month, enterprise plan available and carries moderate lock-in, while Yandex Cloud sits at usage-based pricing, a free tier and carries moderate lock-in. Amazon CloudFront is a fit when you need global — typical scenarios include global audience with low latency needs. Yandex Cloud is aimed at russian and cis-focused businesses needing local cloud with ml capabilities, with workloads like russian public cloud with compute, storage, and ml services as the common case. The honest trade-off: Amazon CloudFront is explicitly not for internal projects; Yandex Cloud is not intended for global startups with western investors or sanctions compliance concerns. On cost specifically, pricing model matters more than sticker price — one workload can make Amazon CloudFront cheaper while a different shape flips the answer to Yandex Cloud.
quick_take: Amazon CloudFront is for global; Yandex Cloud is for russian and cis-focused businesses needing local; decide based on cost model and budget.
verdict_a: Choose Amazon CloudFront if your primary workload is global and the free tier plus $0/month paid tier fits your budget.
verdict_b: Choose Yandex Cloud if your primary workload is russian and cis-focused businesses needing local cloud with ml capabilities.

faq.q: Is Amazon CloudFront cheaper than Yandex Cloud?
faq.a: Amazon CloudFront publishes an entry price of $0/month; Yandex Cloud's entry price is not in our data, so a definitive comparison is not possible without a quote from Yandex Cloud.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Amazon CloudFront to Yandex Cloud?
faq.a: Migrating from Amazon CloudFront to Yandex Cloud is doable but not automatic — Amazon CloudFront carries moderate lock-in. Since both tools sit in the same category (CDN / Edge / Acceleration), the conceptual mapping is closer, but you still need to validate feature parity against your specific workload. Plan migration as a project, not a script: inventory integrations, test on non-production workloads, and keep Amazon CloudFront running in parallel until Yandex Cloud is verified.

Our data gives Amazon CloudFront and Yandex Cloud the same developer experience score (4/5), so neither has a clear edge on DX by that measure. The differentiator is usually how each tool fits your workflow — things like CLI quality, docs, local dev loop, and error messages. The safest call is to pilot both on a representative task and see which one gets out of your way.

faq.q: Is Yandex Cloud a good alternative to Amazon CloudFront?
faq.a: Yandex Cloud is in the same category as Amazon CloudFront (CDN / Edge / Acceleration), so it's a reasonable alternative to evaluate. Yandex Cloud is aimed at russian and cis-focused businesses needing local cloud with ml capabilities, while Amazon CloudFront targets global — that difference should drive the switch decision. Note that Yandex Cloud is explicitly not for global startups with western investors or sanctions compliance concerns — if that describes your workload, it's not a viable replacement.
